name: Community Spirit Credit Union
description_short: Community Spirit Credit Union is a member-owned, non-profit financial cooperative serving residents and employees in Indiana counties since 1937, offering affordable loans, savings accounts, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Community Spirit Credit Union was established in 1937 to serve employees of Joseph E. Seagram & Sons in Lawrenceburg, Indiana and their families. The organization was founded with a dual mission: to provide affordable borrowing options and to promote financial thrift among members. Over the decades, the credit union expanded its membership eligibility beyond the original employee group to serve anyone working or residing in Dearborn, Ohio, Ripley, and Switzerland counties in Indiana, maintaining its commitment to community-focused finance.

As a non-profit, member-owned credit union, Community Spirit offers core financial services including share accounts (savings), credit products, Visa debit and credit cards, and personal loans. Members can access their accounts through a home banking system available on computers and mobile devices, which provides balance reviews, transaction history, fund transfers, and loan payment capabilities. The credit union operates extended hours with after-hours access via ATM, night deposit boxes, and digital banking platforms. They participate in the MoneyPass ATM network, providing fee-free cash withdrawals at participating locations beyond their on-site ATM.

Community Spirit distinguishes itself through its deeply rooted local presence—over 85 years of service to the same regional communities—and its explicit commitment to member welfare over profit maximization. The credit union's tagline, "Where people are worth more than money," reflects its cooperative philosophy. They provide practical member protections including a dedicated lost/stolen card hotline (1-855-510-0955) and travel notification services to prevent fraudulent transaction blocks while traveling. The organization emphasizes accessibility through multiple service channels: in-person, digital, after-hours, and phone support with direct staff email contact.

The main limitation is geographic restriction: membership is limited to those working or residing in specific Indiana counties (Dearborn, Ohio, Ripley, and Switzerland), making it inaccessible to consumers outside these areas. The website provides limited detail on loan products, interest rates, fees, or credit card terms, requiring members to contact the institution directly for specific product information. As a smaller credit union, they may have fewer advanced digital features or product variety compared to larger national credit unions or banks.
services: Visa debit cards with ATM access
Home banking system (online and mobile account access)
Credit card transaction viewing and payment
pros: Non-profit, member-owned structure ensures profits are returned to members rather than shareholders
85+ year history serving the same local communities demonstrates stability and commitment
Free MoneyPass ATM network access for fee-free withdrawals beyond their single on-site ATM
Comprehensive digital banking available 24/7 including home banking, bill pay, and account management
Direct staff contact via email and phone for member questions and after-hours support
Travel notification service helps prevent fraudulent transaction blocks while traveling
Night deposit box for convenient deposits and loan payments outside business hours
cons: Membership is geographically restricted to Dearborn, Ohio, Ripley, and Switzerland counties—not accessible to most U.S. consumers
Limited product information on website; loan terms, rates, fees, and credit card details not disclosed
Single physical location (160 Industrial Drive, Lawrenceburg, IN) limits in-person accessibility for members in distant parts of service area
No mention of NCUA insurance, loan limits, APR ranges, or other standard credit union disclosures on website
Website lacks information on other common credit union services such as certificates of deposit (share certificates), IRAs, or member insurance options
best_for: Employees and residents of Dearborn, Ohio, Ripley, and Switzerland counties in Indiana seeking affordable, community-focused banking
Members who prefer member-owned financial institutions with transparent, non-profit operations
Consumers who value personal service relationships and direct contact with credit union staff
People seeking basic savings, checking, lending, and digital banking services in their local community
diagnosis: Community Spirit Credit Union is best for residents and employees of four specific Indiana counties (Dearborn, Ohio, Ripley, and Switzerland) who seek affordable, member-owned financial services with local roots and personalized service. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility—this institution cannot serve consumers outside its defined service area, and those within it should verify current eligibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Community Spirit Credit Union — NCUA-insured credit union in Lawrenceburg, IN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
